context: Tests whether the agent generates a backup script with the canonical bash safety idioms: env-based shebang, strict mode flags, IFS setting, trap-based cleanup, and error helper functions. The script should never leave partial output on failure.

checklist.name: Env shebang
checklist.description: Shebang line uses `#!/usr/bin/env bash` (not a hardcoded path like #!/bin/bash or #!/usr/bin/bash)

checklist.name: Strict mode set
checklist.description: Script contains `set -euo pipefail` (all three flags present together or individually)

checklist.name: IFS override
checklist.description: Script sets `IFS=$'\n\t'` after the strict mode line

checklist.name: Trap cleanup registered
checklist.description: Script registers a trap on EXIT (and ideally ERR, INT, TERM) that calls a cleanup function

checklist.name: Cleanup removes partial file
checklist.description: The cleanup/trap handler removes any in-progress or partial output file so failed runs do not leave corrupt backups

checklist.name: die() or equivalent helper
checklist.description: Script defines a `die` (or equivalent named) function that prints to stderr and exits non-zero

checklist.name: check_command used
checklist.description: Script verifies that required external commands (e.g. pg_dump, gzip) exist before use, using `command -v` or equivalent

checklist.name: readonly for constants
checklist.description: Script uses `readonly` for at least one script-level constant (e.g. SCRIPT_DIR, SCRIPT_NAME, or a config value)

checklist.name: Logging to stderr
checklist.description: Status/progress messages are written to stderr (>&2), not stdout

checklist.name: Timestamped log messages
checklist.description: Log/status messages include a timestamp (e.g. using `date`)

checklist.name: No hardcoded credentials
checklist.description: Script does NOT contain hardcoded passwords, connection strings, or tokens — relies on environment variables or external config
